name: Semaglutide
subtitle: GLP-1 Receptor Agonist | Weight Loss & Diabetes

modifications: Acylated with C18 fatty diacid via γ-Glu spacer

overview: Long-acting GLP-1 receptor agonist approved for type 2 diabetes and chronic weight management. Over 17,000 trial participants have demonstrated significant efficacy through appetite suppression and glycemic control. The 7-day half-life enables convenient weekly dosing.
mechanism: Mimics native GLP-1, binding to receptors to stimulate glucose-dependent insulin secretion, suppress glucagon, slow gastric emptying, and reduce appetite via hypothalamic pathways.
keyBenefits: Comprehensive safety data from extensive trials

items.name: Clinically Significant Weight Reduction
items.description: FDA-approved for chronic weight management with average 15-20% body weight loss in clinical trials.

items.name: Appetite and Craving Control
items.description: Reduces hunger and food cravings through central nervous system GLP-1 receptor activation.

items.name: Sustained Weight Maintenance
items.description: Long-term studies show maintained weight loss with continued treatment over 2+ years.

items.name: Glycemic Control
items.description: FDA-approved for type 2 diabetes with HbA1c reductions of 1.5-2% in clinical trials.

items.name: Cardiovascular Protection
items.description: Proven 26% reduction in cardiovascular death, MI, or stroke in high-risk diabetes patients.

items.name: Beta Cell Preservation
items.description: Improves insulin secretion and may preserve pancreatic beta cell function.

items.name: Metabolic Syndrome Improvement
items.description: Addresses multiple components: weight, glucose, blood pressure, and lipids.

items.name: NASH/Fatty Liver
items.description: Emerging evidence for improvements in non-alcoholic fatty liver disease.

items.name: Inflammation Reduction
items.description: Decreases systemic inflammation markers independent of weight loss.

deliveryMethods.overview: Pre-filled pen devices for once-weekly subcutaneous injection. Brands include Ozempic (diabetes) and Wegovy (weight loss).

steps: If using pre-filled pen, attach new needle and prime per instructions
If using vial, draw prescribed dose with appropriate syringe
Clean injection site with alcohol swab and let dry
Inject subcutaneously at 90-degree angle (45-degree if lean)
Hold for 6 seconds after injection to ensure full dose delivery
Dispose of needle safely and rotate injection sites weekly

keyBenefits: Abdomen, thigh, or upper arm injection sites

deliveryMethods.overview: Rybelsus tablets use SNAC technology for enhanced absorption. Requires strict administration protocol.

steps: Take first thing in morning on completely empty stomach
Swallow tablet whole with no more than 4 ounces plain water
Do not crush, chew, or split tablet
Wait at least 30 minutes before eating, drinking, or taking other medications
After 30 minutes, resume normal activities and meals
Take at same time each day for consistency

interactions.notes: May increase hypoglycemia risk when combined; requires blood glucose monitoring.
Both are incretin mimetics with overlapping mechanisms; concurrent use increases side effects.
Combined as CagriSema in clinical trials for enhanced weight loss efficacy.
Commonly used together for diabetes management with good safety profile.
No contraindications; may help with GI side effects.
Increased hypoglycemia risk; may require sulfonylurea dose reduction.
Delayed gastric emptying may affect absorption of oral medications.

timeline.effects: Mild appetite reduction, possible nausea during initial dose
Noticeable weight loss (5-10% typical), improved satiety
Continued weight loss (10-15% common), stable glucose levels
Weight loss plateau possible, focus on maintenance

stopSigns: Signs of thyroid tumor (neck lump, hoarseness, trouble swallowing)
Severe allergic reaction (rash, itching, difficulty breathing)
contraindications: Personal or family history of medullary thyroid cancer

good: Proper refrigeration verified and not expired
Clear, colorless to slightly yellow solution in pen/vial
warning: Compounded versions - FDA warns about untested compounded semaglutide
bad: Non-pharmacy sources or unverified online sellers

references.title: Once-Weekly Semaglutide in Adults with Overweight or Obesity (STEP 1)
references.keyFindings: 14.9% average weight loss vs 2.4% placebo over 68 weeks with 2.4mg weekly. 86% achieved ≥5% weight loss; 51-64% achieved ≥15% weight loss.

references.title: Semaglutide and Cardiovascular Outcomes in Obesity without Diabetes (SELECT)
references.keyFindings: 20% reduction in MACE (CV death, MI, or stroke) with semaglutide 2.4mg vs placebo over mean 40 months. First GLP-1 RA to demonstrate cardiovascular benefit in non-diabetic population.

references.title: Semaglutide and Cardiovascular Outcomes in Patients with Type 2 Diabetes (SUSTAIN-6)
references.participants: 3,297 patients with type 2 diabetes at high CV risk
references.keyFindings: 26% reduction in MACE (HR 0.74) with semaglutide vs placebo. Established cardiovascular safety and benefit profile for semaglutide in type 2 diabetes.

references.title: Two-year effects of semaglutide in adults with overweight or obesity (STEP 5)
references.keyFindings: Sustained weight loss of 15.2% at week 104 vs 2.6% placebo. 77.1% achieved ≥5% weight loss at 2 years, demonstrating long-term efficacy.

references.title: Oral Semaglutide and Cardiovascular Outcomes in Patients with Type 2 Diabetes (PIONEER 6)
references.participants: 3,183 patients with type 2 diabetes at high CV risk
references.keyFindings: Oral semaglutide was noninferior to placebo for cardiovascular safety. Numerically fewer CV deaths in oral semaglutide group (0.9% vs 1.9%).

references.title: Once-Weekly Semaglutide in Adolescents with Obesity (STEP TEENS)
references.keyFindings: 16.1% BMI reduction with semaglutide vs 0.6% increase with placebo over 68 weeks. 44.9% of semaglutide recipients reclassified to normal-weight or overweight BMI category.

references.title: Semaglutide in Patients with Heart Failure with Preserved Ejection Fraction and Obesity (STEP-HFpEF)
references.keyFindings: Semaglutide 2.4mg reduced symptoms, physical limitations, and improved exercise function in obesity-related HFpEF. Reduced inflammation and appeared to improve adverse cardiac remodeling.

references.title: Effects of Semaglutide on Chronic Kidney Disease in Patients with Type 2 Diabetes (FLOW)
references.keyFindings: 24% lower risk of primary kidney outcome (kidney failure, sustained eGFR decline, or renal/CV death) with semaglutide vs placebo. 29% reduction in CV death. Trial stopped early for efficacy.

references.title: Phase 3 Trial of Semaglutide in Metabolic Dysfunction-Associated Steatohepatitis (ESSENCE)
references.participants: 1,197 patients with MASH and fibrosis stage 2-3
references.keyFindings: 62.9% achieved MASH resolution without fibrosis worsening vs 34.3% placebo at 72 weeks. Combined resolution and fibrosis improvement in 32.7% vs 16.1% placebo. Mean weight loss of 10.5%.

latestResearch.title: ESSENCE Phase 3 MASH Trial Results
latestResearch.summary: NEJM published interim results showing semaglutide 2.4mg achieved MASH resolution in 62.9% of patients with moderate-to-advanced liver fibrosis, establishing efficacy in metabolic liver disease.

latestResearch.title: FLOW Kidney Outcomes Trial
latestResearch.summary: Semaglutide reduced kidney disease progression by 24% and CV death by 29% in patients with T2DM and CKD. Trial stopped early for efficacy — first GLP-1 RA to demonstrate kidney-protective benefits.

latestResearch.title: SELECT Cardiovascular Outcomes
latestResearch.summary: Landmark trial showing 20% MACE reduction with semaglutide 2.4mg in 17,604 patients with obesity and CVD but without diabetes, expanding the cardiovascular indication beyond diabetic populations.

quickStats.storage: Pen: 2-8°C before first use, room temp up to 56 days after. Compounded: 2-8°C
